   113→
   114→// calculateTimedOut computes the number of sessions that timed out during sync.
   115→// This is calculated as: attempted - (skipped + updated + created + errored)
   116→// Includes defensive validation to prevent negative values in case of counter bugs.
   117→func (stats *CloudSyncStats) calculateTimedOut() {
   118→	finished := stats.SessionsSkipped + stats.SessionsUpdated + stats.SessionsCreated + stats.SessionsErrored
   119→
   120→	// Defensive validation: finished should never exceed attempted
   121→	if finished > stats.SessionsAttempted {
   122→		slog.Error("CloudSyncStats counter inconsistency detected",
   123→			"attempted", stats.SessionsAttempted,
   124→			"finished", finished,
   125→			"skipped", stats.SessionsSkipped,
   126→			"updated", stats.SessionsUpdated,
   127→			"created", stats.SessionsCreated,
   128→			"errored", stats.SessionsErrored)
   129→		stats.SessionsTimedOut = 0
   130→		return
   131→	}
   132→
   133→	stats.SessionsTimedOut = stats.SessionsAttempted - finished
   134→}
   135→
   136→// sessionDebounceState tracks debounce state for a single session
   137→type sessionDebounceState struct {
   138→	mu           sync.Mutex          // Protects concurrent access to this session's debounce state
   139→	lastSyncTime time.Time           // Timestamp when last sync started (used to calculate debounce window)
   140→	pending      *pendingSyncRequest // Most recent sync request queued during debounce window (newer requests replace older)
   141→	timer        *time.Timer         // Fires after debounce interval to flush pending sync (nil if no pending sync)
   142→}
   143→
   144→// SyncManager handles cloud synchronization operations
   145→type SyncManager struct {
   146→	enabled          bool
   147→	wg               sync.WaitGroup
   148→	silent           bool
   149→	syncCount        int32          // Atomic counter for active syncs
   150→	httpSemaphore    chan struct{}  // Semaphore to limit concurrent HTTP requests
   151→	stats            CloudSyncStats // Statistics for the current sync operation
   152→	debounceInterval time.Duration  // Debounce interval for run mode (default: SessionDebounceInterval)
   153→	debounceSessions sync.Map       // Maps sessionID -> *sessionDebounceState
   154→	bulkSizes        map[string]int // Optional: sessionID -> size cache for batch operations
   155→	bulkSizesMu      sync.RWMutex   // Protects bulkSizes map
   156→}
   157→
   158→var (
   159→	globalSyncManager *SyncManager
   160→	syncManagerMutex  sync.RWMutex
   161→	deviceID          string         // Cached device ID
   162→	clientVersion     string = "dev" // Will be set from main
   163→	apiBaseURL        string         // Base URL for API calls
   164→)
   165→
   166→// incrementCategory atomically increments the counter for a given category
   167→func (stats *CloudSyncStats) incrementCategory(category syncCategory) {
   168→	switch category {
   169→	case categorySkipped:
   170→		atomic.AddInt32(&stats.SessionsSkipped, 1)
   171→	case categoryErrored:
   172→		atomic.AddInt32(&stats.SessionsErrored, 1)
   173→	case categoryUpdated:
   174→		atomic.AddInt32(&stats.SessionsUpdated, 1)
   175→	case categoryCreated:
   176→		atomic.AddInt32(&stats.SessionsCreated, 1)
   177→	}
   178→}
   179→
   180→// decrementCategory atomically decrements the counter for a given category
   181→func (stats *CloudSyncStats) decrementCategory(category syncCategory) {
   182→	switch category {
   183→	case categorySkipped:
   184→		atomic.AddInt32(&stats.SessionsSkipped, -1)
   185→	case categoryErrored:
   186→		atomic.AddInt32(&stats.SessionsErrored, -1)
   187→	case categoryUpdated:
   188→		atomic.AddInt32(&stats.SessionsUpdated, -1)
   189→	case categoryCreated:
   190→		atomic.AddInt32(&stats.SessionsCreated, -1)
   191→	}
   192→}
   193→
   194→// shouldReplaceCategory determines if newCategory should replace oldCategory based on precedence
   195→// Precedence (highest to lowest): created > updated > errored > skipped
   196→func shouldReplaceCategory(oldCategory, newCategory syncCategory) bool {
   197→	// Created never gets replaced
   198→	if oldCategory == categoryCreated {
   199→		return false
   200→	}
   201→	// Updated replaces skipped and errored
   202→	if newCategory == categoryUpdated {
   203→		return oldCategory == categorySkipped || oldCategory == categoryErrored
   204→	}
   205→	// Errored replaces only skipped
   206→	if newCategory == categoryErrored {
   207→		return oldCategory == categorySkipped
   208→	}
   209→	// Created replaces everything (but we already handled oldCategory == created above)
   210→	if newCategory == categoryCreated {
   211→		return true
   212→	}
   213→	// Skipped doesn't replace anything
   214→	return false
   215→}
   216→
   217→// trackSessionInCategory records a session in a category and updates counters atomically
   218→// Handles precedence rules and automatically increments/decrements appropriate counters
   219→func (stats *CloudSyncStats) trackSessionInCategory(sessionID string, newCategory syncCategory) {
   220→	for {
   221→		// Try to load existing category
   222→		existing, loaded := stats.countedSessions.Load(sessionID)
   223→
   224→		if !loaded {
   225→			// No existing entry, try to store
   226→			actual, loaded := stats.countedSessions.LoadOrStore(sessionID, newCategory)
   227→			if !loaded {
   228→				// Successfully stored new category - increment counter
   229→				stats.incrementCategory(newCategory)
   230→				return
   231→			}
   232→			// Someone else stored it first, loop to check precedence
   233→			existing = actual
   234→		}
   235→
   236→		oldCategory := existing.(syncCategory)
   237→
   238→		// Check if we should replace the old category
   239→		if !shouldReplaceCategory(oldCategory, newCategory) {
   240→			// Don't replace - higher precedence category already exists
   241→			return
   242→		}
   243→
   244→		// Try to replace old category with new category
   245→		if stats.countedSessions.CompareAndSwap(sessionID, oldCategory, newCategory) {
   246→			// Successfully replaced - decrement old counter and increment new counter
   247→			stats.decrementCategory(oldCategory)
   248→			stats.incrementCategory(newCategory)
   249→			return
   250→		}
   251→		// CompareAndSwap failed, someone else modified it, loop again
   252→	}
   253→}
   254→
   255→// acquireHTTPSemaphore acquires the HTTP semaphore (blocks until available)
   256→// Returns a release function that should be called with defer
   257→//
   258→// No timeout - blocks indefinitely until semaphore is available. Goroutines that don't acquire
   259→// before the overall sync timeout (CloudSyncTimeout) are orphaned, but this is acceptable because
   260→// the process terminates after sync timeout. The overall timeout handles cleanup and stats reporting.
   261→func (syncMgr *SyncManager) acquireHTTPSemaphore(sessionID, requestType string) (releaseFunc func()) {
   262→	// Block until semaphore slot is available
   263→	syncMgr.httpSemaphore <- struct{}{}
   264→
   265→	slog.Debug("Acquired HTTP semaphore",
   266→		"sessionId", sessionID,
   267→		"requestType", requestType,
   268→		"currentConcurrency", len(syncMgr.httpSemaphore))
   269→
   270→	releaseFunc = func() {
   271→		<-syncMgr.httpSemaphore
   272→		slog.Debug("Released HTTP semaphore",
   273→			"sessionId", sessionID,
   274→			"requestType", requestType,
   275→			"currentConcurrency", len(syncMgr.httpSemaphore))
   276→	}
   277→	return releaseFunc
   278→}
   279→
   280→// fetchBulkSessionSizes fetches markdown sizes for all sessions in a project from the SpecStory Cloud API.
   281→// This enables batch sync optimization by avoiding individual HEAD requests for each session.
   282→//
   283→// The function makes a GET request to /api/v1/projects/{projectID}/sessions/sizes with automatic
   284→// retry logic (3 attempts with 1 second delay between retries) to handle transient network failures.
   285→// This function is only called once during batch sync initialization, so no HTTP semaphore is needed.
   286→//
   287→// Parameters:
   288→//   - projectID: The project identifier (git_id, workspace_id, or directory name)
   289→//
   290→// Returns:
   291→//   - map[string]int: Map of sessionID to markdown content size in bytes. Empty map if project has no sessions.
   292→//   - error: Non-nil if all retry attempts fail, including request creation, network, parsing, or API errors.
   293→//
   294→// The function logs at Debug level for normal operation and Error level for failures. On success, it logs
   295→// at Info level with the count of sessions fetched.
   296→func (syncMgr *SyncManager) fetchBulkSessionSizes(projectID string) (map[string]int, error) {
   297→	const maxAttempts = 3
   298→	const retryDelay = 1 * time.Second
   299→
   300→	apiURL := GetAPIBaseURL() + "/api/v1/projects/" + projectID + "/sessions/sizes"
   301→
   302→	for attempt := 1; attempt <= maxAttempts; attempt++ {
   303→		// Log attempt
   304→		if attempt == 1 {
   305→			slog.Debug("Fetching bulk session sizes",
   306→				"projectId", projectID,
   307→				"url", apiURL,
   308→				"attempt", attempt)
   309→		} else {
   310→			slog.Debug("Retrying bulk session sizes fetch",
   311→				"projectId", projectID,
   312→				"url", apiURL,
   313→				"attempt", attempt)
   314→		}
   315→
   316→		// Make GET request
   317→		req, err := http.NewRequest("GET", apiURL, nil)
   318→		if err != nil {
   319→			slog.Error("Failed to create GET request for bulk sizes",
   320→				"projectId", projectID,
   321→				"attempt", attempt,
   322→				"error", err)
   323→			if attempt < maxAttempts {
   324→				time.Sleep(retryDelay)
   325→				continue
   326→			}
   327→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to create request after %d attempts: %w", maxAttempts, err)
   328→		}
   329→
   330→		// Add auth headers
   331→		cloudToken := GetCloudToken()
   332→		req.Header.Set("Authorization", "Bearer "+cloudToken)
   333→		req.Header.Set("User-Agent", GetUserAgent())
   334→
   335→		// Create HTTP client with timeout
   336→		client := &http.Client{
   337→			Timeout: 30 * time.Second,
   338→		}
   339→
   340→		// Execute request
   341→		resp, err := client.Do(req)
   342→
   343→		if err != nil {
   344→			slog.Debug("Bulk sizes GET request failed",
   345→				"projectId", projectID,
   346→				"attempt", attempt,
   347→				"error", err)
   348→			if attempt < maxAttempts {
   349→				time.Sleep(retryDelay)
   350→				continue
   351→			}
   352→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("GET request failed after %d attempts: %w", maxAttempts, err)
   353→		}
   354→
   355→		// Read response body
   356→		defer func() { _ = resp.Body.Close() }()
   357→		respBody, err := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)
   358→		if err != nil {
   359→			slog.Error("Failed to read bulk sizes response body",
   360→				"projectId", projectID,
   361→				"attempt", attempt,
   362→				"status", resp.StatusCode,
   363→				"error", err)
   364→			if attempt < maxAttempts {
   365→				time.Sleep(retryDelay)
   366→				continue
   367→			}
   368→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to read response body after %d attempts: %w", maxAttempts, err)
   369→		}
   370→
   371→		// Check status code
   372→		if resp.StatusCode != 200 {
   373→			slog.Debug("Bulk sizes GET returned non-200 status",
   374→				"projectId", projectID,
   375→				"attempt", attempt,
   376→				"status", resp.StatusCode,
   377→				"responseBody", string(respBody))
   378→			if attempt < maxAttempts {
   379→				time.Sleep(retryDelay)
   380→				continue
   381→			}
   382→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("GET request returned status %d after %d attempts", resp.StatusCode, maxAttempts)
   383→		}
   384→
   385→		// Parse JSON response
   386→		var bulkResponse BulkSizesResponse
   387→		err = json.Unmarshal(respBody, &bulkResponse)
   388→		if err != nil {
   389→			slog.Error("Failed to parse bulk sizes JSON response",
   390→				"projectId", projectID,
   391→				"attempt", attempt,
   392→				"error", err,
   393→				"responseBody", string(respBody))
   394→			if attempt < maxAttempts {
   395→				time.Sleep(retryDelay)
   396→				continue
   397→			}
   398→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to parse JSON after %d attempts: %w", maxAttempts, err)
   399→		}
   400→
   401→		// Check success field
   402→		if !bulkResponse.Success {
   403→			slog.Warn("Bulk sizes response indicated failure",
   404→				"projectId", projectID,
   405→				"attempt", attempt)
   406→			if attempt < maxAttempts {
   407→				time.Sleep(retryDelay)
   408→				continue
   409→			}
   410→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("API returned success=false after %d attempts", maxAttempts)
   411→		}
   412→
   413→		// Success - log and return
   414→		sessionCount := len(bulkResponse.Data.Sessions)
   415→		slog.Info("Fetched bulk session sizes",
   416→			"projectId", projectID,
   417→			"sessionCount", sessionCount)
   418→		return bulkResponse.Data.Sessions, nil
   419→	}
   420→
   421→	// Should never reach here, but just in case
   422→	return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to fetch bulk sizes after %d attempts", maxAttempts)
   423→}
   424→
   425→// PreloadSessionSizes fetches and caches session sizes for all sessions in a project
   426→// This is a blocking call that should be made before syncing multiple sessions
   427→// If fetch fails, logs warning and leaves cache nil (enables HEAD request fallback)
   428→func (syncMgr *SyncManager) PreloadSessionSizes(projectID string) {
   429→	if syncMgr == nil {
   430→		slog.Warn("PreloadSessionSizes called with nil sync manager")
   431→		return
   432→	}
   433→
   434→	slog.Debug("Preloading session sizes for batch sync",
   435→		"projectId", projectID)
   436→
   437→	// Fetch bulk sizes (with retry logic)
   438→	sizes, err := syncMgr.fetchBulkSessionSizes(projectID)
   439→	if err != nil {
   440→		// Log error and leave cache nil to trigger HEAD request fallback
   441→		slog.Error("Failed to fetch bulk session sizes, will fall back to HEAD requests",
   442→			"projectId", projectID,
   443→			"error", err)
   444→		return
   445→	}
   446→
   447→	// Store in cache with write lock
   448→	syncMgr.bulkSizesMu.Lock()
   449→	defer syncMgr.bulkSizesMu.Unlock()
   450→	syncMgr.bulkSizes = sizes
   451→
   452→	sessionCount := len(sizes)
   453→	slog.Info("Preloaded session sizes from server",
   454→		"projectId", projectID,
   455→		"sessionCount", sessionCount)
   456→}
   457→
   458→// requiresSync checks if a session needs to be synced to the cloud
   459→// Returns true if sync is needed, false if already up-to-date
   460→func (syncMgr *SyncManager) requiresSync(sessionID, mdPath, mdContent, projectID string, skipHeadCheck bool) (bool, error) {
   461→	// Skip HEAD check if requested (caller determined sync is needed)
   462→	if skipHeadCheck {
   463→		slog.Debug("Skipping HEAD check (skipHeadCheck=true)",
   464→			"sessionId", sessionID)
   465→		return true, nil
   466→	}
   467→
   468→	// Get size from passed content
   469→	localSize := len(mdContent)
   470→
   471→	// Check if bulk sizes are preloaded (batch sync mode)
   472→	syncMgr.bulkSizesMu.RLock()
   473→	bulkSizes := syncMgr.bulkSizes
   474→	syncMgr.bulkSizesMu.RUnlock()
   475→
   476→	if bulkSizes != nil {
   477→		// Bulk sizes are available - use cached data instead of HEAD request
   478→		serverSize, exists := bulkSizes[sessionID]
   479→		if !exists {
   480→			// Session not in bulk sizes map - doesn't exist on server yet
   481→			slog.Debug("Using preloaded size for sync check: session not on server",
   482→				"sessionId", sessionID,
   483→				"localSize", localSize)
   484→			return true, nil
   485→		}
   486→
   487→		// Compare sizes
   488→		needsSync := localSize > serverSize
   489→		if needsSync {
   490→			slog.Debug("Using preloaded size for sync check: local is larger",
   491→				"sessionId", sessionID,
   492→				"localSize", localSize,
   493→				"serverSize", serverSize)
   494→		} else {
   495→			slog.Info("Session already up-to-date on server (using preloaded sizes), skipping sync",
   496→				"sessionId", sessionID,
   497→				"localSize", localSize,
   498→				"serverSize", serverSize)
   499→		}
   500→		return needsSync, nil
   501→	}
   502→
   503→	// Bulk sizes not available - fall back to HEAD request
   504→	slog.Debug("Using HEAD request for sync check (no bulk sizes)",
   505→		"sessionId", sessionID)
   506→
   507→	// Acquire semaphore for HEAD request (blocks until available)
   508→	release := syncMgr.acquireHTTPSemaphore(sessionID, "HEAD")
   509→	defer release()
   510→
   511→	// Make HEAD request
   512→	apiURL := GetAPIBaseURL() + "/api/v1/projects/" + projectID + "/sessions/" + sessionID
   513→	req, err := http.NewRequest("HEAD", apiURL, nil)
   514→	if err != nil {
   515→		slog.Error("Failed to create HEAD request",
   516→			"sessionId", sessionID,
   517→			"error", err)
   518→		return false, err
   519→	}
   520→
   521→	// Add auth headers
   522→	cloudToken := GetCloudToken()
   523→	req.Header.Set("Authorization", "Bearer "+cloudToken)
   524→	req.Header.Set("User-Agent", GetUserAgent())
   525→
   526→	// Create HTTP client with shorter timeout for HEAD
   527→	client := &http.Client{
   528→		Timeout: 10 * time.Second,
   529→	}
   530→
   531→	slog.Debug("Cloud sync HEAD request",
   532→		"sessionId", sessionID,
   533→		"url", apiURL,
   534→		"localMarkdownSize", localSize)
   535→
   536→	// Execute request
   537→	resp, err := client.Do(req)
   538→	if err != nil {
   539→		slog.Debug("HEAD request failed, will attempt sync",
   540→			"sessionId", sessionID,
   541→			"error", err)
   542→		return true, nil // Err on side of syncing if HEAD fails
   543→	}
   544→	defer func() { _ = resp.Body.Close() }()
   545→
   546→	slog.Debug("Cloud sync HEAD response",
   547→		"sessionId", sessionID,
   548→		"status", resp.StatusCode,
   549→		"headers", resp.Header)
   550→
   551→	// Check response
   552→	if resp.StatusCode == 404 {
   553→		slog.Debug("Session not found on server, will sync",
   554→			"sessionId", sessionID)
   555→		return true, nil
   556→	}
   557→
   558→	if resp.StatusCode == 200 {
   559→		serverSizeStr := resp.Header.Get("X-Markdown-Size")
   560→		if serverSizeStr == "" {
   561→			slog.Debug("No X-Markdown-Size header, will sync",
   562→				"sessionId", sessionID)
   563→			return true, nil
   564→		}
   565→
   566→		serverSize, err := strconv.Atoi(serverSizeStr)
   567→		if err != nil {
   568→			slog.Debug("Invalid X-Markdown-Size header, will sync",
   569→				"sessionId", sessionID,
   570→				"headerValue", serverSizeStr,
   571→				"error", err)
   572→			return true, nil
   573→		}
   574→
   575→		if localSize > serverSize {
   576→			slog.Debug("Local markdown larger than server, will sync",
   577→				"sessionId", sessionID,
   578→				"localSize", localSize,
   579→				"serverSize", serverSize)
   580→			return true, nil
   581→		}
   582→
   583→		slog.Info("Session already up-to-date on server, skipping sync",
   584→			"sessionId", sessionID,
   585→			"localSize", localSize,
   586→			"serverSize", serverSize)
   587→		return false, nil
   588→	}
   589→
   590→	// For any other status codes, err on side of syncing
   591→	slog.Debug("Unexpected HEAD response status, will sync",
   592→		"sessionId", sessionID,
   593→		"status", resp.StatusCode)
   594→	return true, nil
   595→}
   596→
   597→// SetClientVersion sets the client version for API requests
   598→func SetClientVersion(version string) {
   599→	clientVersion = version
   600→}
   601→
   602→// GetClientVersion returns the current client version
   603→func GetClientVersion() string {
   604→	return clientVersion
   605→}
   606→
   607→// SetAPIBaseURL sets the base URL for API requests
   608→func SetAPIBaseURL(url string) {
   609→	apiBaseURL = url
   610→}
   611→
   612→// GetAPIBaseURL returns the base URL for API requests
   613→func GetAPIBaseURL() string {
   614→	if apiBaseURL == "" {
   615→		return "https://cloud.specstory.com" // Default API base URL
   616→	}
   617→	return apiBaseURL
   618→}
   619→
   620→// GetUserAgent returns the User-Agent string for API requests
   621→func GetUserAgent() string {
   622→	return fmt.Sprintf("%s/%s SpecStory, Inc.", ClientName, clientVersion)
   623→}
   624→
   625→// getDeviceID generates a unique device ID based on MAC address
   626→func getDeviceID() string {
   627→	// Return cached value if available
   628→	if deviceID != "" {
   629→		return deviceID
   630→	}
   631→
   632→	// Get all network interfaces
   633→	interfaces, err := net.Interfaces()
   634→	if err != nil {
   635→		slog.Warn("Failed to get network interfaces", "error", err)
   636→		// Fallback to hostname-based ID
   637→		hostname, _ := os.Hostname()
   638→		h := sha256.Sum256([]byte("specstory-" + hostname))
   639→		deviceID = hex.EncodeToString(h[:])
   640→		return deviceID
   641→	}
   642→
   643→	// Find the first non-loopback interface with a MAC address
   644→	for _, iface := range interfaces {
   645→		if iface.Flags&net.FlagLoopback == 0 && len(iface.HardwareAddr) > 0 {
   646→			// Hash the MAC address
   647→			h := sha256.Sum256(iface.HardwareAddr)
   648→			deviceID = hex.EncodeToString(h[:])
   649→			slog.Debug("Generated device ID from interface", "interface", iface.Name)
   650→			return deviceID
   651→		}
   652→	}
   653→
   654→	// Fallback if no suitable interface found
   655→	hostname, _ := os.Hostname()
   656→	h := sha256.Sum256([]byte("specstory-fallback-" + hostname))
   657→	deviceID = hex.EncodeToString(h[:])
   658→	slog.Debug("Using fallback device ID based on hostname")
   659→	return deviceID
   660→}
   661→
   662→// InitSyncManager initializes the global sync manager
   663→func InitSyncManager(enabled bool) {
   664→	syncManagerMutex.Lock()
   665→	defer syncManagerMutex.Unlock()
   666→	globalSyncManager = &SyncManager{
   667→		enabled:          enabled,
   668→		silent:           false,
   669→		httpSemaphore:    make(chan struct{}, MaxConcurrentHTTPRequests),
   670→		debounceInterval: SessionDebounceInterval,
   671→	}
   672→	slog.Debug("Cloud sync manager initialized", "enabled", enabled, "maxConcurrentHTTPRequests", MaxConcurrentHTTPRequests)
   673→}
   674→
   675→// SetSilent configures whether to show user feedback during shutdown
   676→func SetSilent(silent bool) {
   677→	syncManagerMutex.Lock()
   678→	defer syncManagerMutex.Unlock()
   679→	if globalSyncManager != nil {
   680→		globalSyncManager.silent = silent
   681→	}
   682→}
   683→
   684→// GetSyncManager returns the global sync manager
   685→func GetSyncManager() *SyncManager {
   686→	syncManagerMutex.RLock()
   687→	defer syncManagerMutex.RUnlock()
   688→	return globalSyncManager
   689→}
   690→
   691→// performSync executes the actual sync operation (HEAD check + PUT request)
   692→func (syncMgr *SyncManager) performSync(sessionID, mdPath, mdContent string, rawData []byte, agentName string, skipHeadCheck bool) {
   693→	timestamp := time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
   694→
   695→	// Log the sync attempt
   696→	slog.Info("Cloud sync initiated",
   697→		"sessionId", sessionID,
   698→		"mdPath", mdPath,
   699→		"agentName", agentName,
   700→		"timestamp", timestamp)
   701→
   702→	// Track that this session attempted to sync (for timeout calculation at shutdown)
   703→	// Only count each unique session once, even if performSync is called multiple times
   704→	// (e.g., due to debouncing in run mode triggering multiple syncs for the same session)
   705→	// Note: We check the map here but don't store yet - trackSessionInCategory() (called
   706→	// later in this function on every code path) will store the session in the map
   707→	if _, alreadyTracked := syncMgr.stats.countedSessions.Load(sessionID); !alreadyTracked {
   708→		atomic.AddInt32(&syncMgr.stats.SessionsAttempted, 1)
   709→	}
   710→
   711→	// Content is now passed in as parameters, no need to read files
   712→
   713→	// Create sync session data
   714→	syncData := SyncSession{
   715→		SessionID:    sessionID,
   716→		MDPath:       mdPath,
   717→		JSONLPath:    "", // No longer using file path
   718→		JSONLContent: rawData,
   719→		MDContent:    mdContent,
   720→	}
   721→
   722→	// Read project configuration
   723→	projectData, err := readProjectConfig()
   724→	if err != nil {
   725→		slog.Warn("Cannot sync session to cloud: failed to read project config", "error", err, "sessionId", sessionID)
   726→		syncMgr.stats.trackSessionInCategory(sessionID, categoryErrored)
   727→		return
   728→	}
   729→
   730→	// Determine project ID - use git_id if present, otherwise workspace_id
   731→	projectID := ""
   732→	if projectData != nil && projectData.GitID != "" {
   733→		projectID = projectData.GitID
   734→	} else if projectData != nil && projectData.WorkspaceID != "" {
   735→		projectID = projectData.WorkspaceID
   736→	}
   737→
   738→	// Project ID is required for cloud sync
   739→	if projectID == "" {
   740→		slog.Warn("Cannot sync session to cloud: no project ID available", "sessionId", sessionID)
   741→		syncMgr.stats.trackSessionInCategory(sessionID, categoryErrored)
   742→		return
   743→	}
   744→
   745→	// Get project name (use directory name as fallback - this is cosmetic only)
   746→	projectName := ""
   747→	if projectData != nil && projectData.ProjectName != "" {
   748→		projectName = projectData.ProjectName
   749→	} else {
   750→		// Fallback to extracting from mdPath for display purposes only
   751→		dir := filepath.Dir(filepath.Dir(mdPath))      // Go up from history to .specstory
   752→		projectName = filepath.Base(filepath.Dir(dir)) // Get the project directory name
   753→		slog.Debug("No project name in config, using directory name", "projectName", projectName)
   754→	}
   755→
   756→	// Check if sync is needed using HEAD request
   757→	shouldSync, err := syncMgr.requiresSync(sessionID, mdPath, mdContent, projectID, skipHeadCheck)
   758→	if err != nil {
   759→		slog.Error("Failed to check if sync needed",
   760→			"sessionId", sessionID,
   761→			"error", err)
   762→		// Continue with sync attempt anyway
   763→	} else if !shouldSync {
   764→		// Track skipped session with precedence rules
   765→		syncMgr.stats.trackSessionInCategory(sessionID, categorySkipped)
   766→		slog.Info("Skipping cloud sync, already up-to-date",
   767→			"sessionId", sessionID)
   768→		return
   769→	}
   770→
   771→	// Extract name from markdown filename (without path or extension)
   772→	name := filepath.Base(mdPath)
   773→	if ext := filepath.Ext(name); ext != "" {
   774→		name = name[:len(name)-len(ext)]
   775→	}
   776→
   777→	// Create API request
   778→	metadata := APIRequestMetadata{
   779→		ClientName:    ClientName,
   780→		ClientVersion: clientVersion,
   781→		AgentName:     agentName, // Use the passed-in agent name
   782→		DeviceID:      getDeviceID(),
   783→	}
   784→	apiReq := APIRequest{
   785→		ProjectID:   projectID,
   786→		ProjectName: projectName,
   787→		Name:        name,
   788→		Markdown:    syncData.MDContent,
   789→		RawData:     string(syncData.JSONLContent),
   790→		Metadata:    metadata,
   791→	}
   792→
   793→	// Marshal to JSON
   794→	jsonData, err := json.Marshal(apiReq)
   795→	if err != nil {
   796→		slog.Error("Cloud sync error marshaling JSON", "sessionId", sessionID, "error", err)
   797→		return
   798→	}
   799→
   800→	// Acquire semaphore for PUT request (blocks until available)
   801→	release := syncMgr.acquireHTTPSemaphore(sessionID, "PUT")
   802→	defer release()
   803→
   804→	// Make HTTP request
   805→	apiURL := GetAPIBaseURL() + "/api/v1/projects/" + projectID + "/sessions/" + sessionID
   806→	req, err := http.NewRequest("PUT", apiURL, bytes.NewBuffer(jsonData))
   807→	if err != nil {
   808→		slog.Error("Cloud sync error creating request", "sessionId", sessionID, "error", err)
   809→		return
   810→	}
   811→
   812→	// Get cloud token for authorization
   813→	cloudToken := GetCloudToken()
   814→	req.Header.Set("Authorization", "Bearer "+cloudToken)
   815→	req.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
   816→	req.Header.Set("Accept", "application/json")
   817→	req.Header.Set("User-Agent", GetUserAgent())
   818→
   819→	// Create HTTP client with timeout
   820→	client := &http.Client{
   821→		Timeout: 30 * time.Second,
   822→	}
   823→
   824→	slog.Debug("Cloud sync making API call",
   825→		"sessionId", sessionID,
   826→		"url", apiURL,
   827→		"projectId", projectID,
   828→		"projectName", projectName,
   829→		"name", name,
   830→		"metadata", metadata,
   831→		"jsonlSize", len(syncData.JSONLContent),
   832→		"mdSize", len(syncData.MDContent),
   833→		"deviceId", getDeviceID())
   834→
   835→	// Make the request
   836→	resp, err := client.Do(req)
   837→	if err != nil {
   838→		// Track network/request errors with precedence rules
   839→		syncMgr.stats.trackSessionInCategory(sessionID, categoryErrored)
   840→		slog.Error("Cloud sync API error", "sessionId", sessionID, "error", err)
   841→		return
   842→	}
   843→	defer func() { _ = resp.Body.Close() }()
   844→
   845→	// Check response status
   846→	if resp.StatusCode >= 200 && resp.StatusCode < 300 {
   847→		// Get ETag from response header
   848→		etag := resp.Header.Get("ETag")
   849→		slog.Info("Cloud sync completed successfully",
   850→			"sessionId", sessionID,
   851→			"status", resp.StatusCode,
   852→			"etag", etag)
   853→
   854→		// Track as created or updated based on status code with precedence rules
   855→		if resp.StatusCode == 201 {
   856→			syncMgr.stats.trackSessionInCategory(sessionID, categoryCreated)
   857→		} else {
   858→			syncMgr.stats.trackSessionInCategory(sessionID, categoryUpdated)
   859→		}
   860→	} else {
   861→		// Track as error with precedence rules
   862→		syncMgr.stats.trackSessionInCategory(sessionID, categoryErrored)
   863→
   864→		// Try to read error response body
   865→		respBody, err := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)
   866→		if err != nil {
   867→			slog.Error("Cloud sync API returned error",
   868→				"sessionId", sessionID,
   869→				"status", resp.StatusCode,
   870→				"bodyReadError", err)
   871→		} else {
   872→			slog.Error("Cloud sync API returned error",
   873→				"sessionId", sessionID,
   874→				"status", resp.StatusCode,
   875→				"responseBody", string(respBody))
   876→		}
   877→	}
   878→}
   879→
   880→// debouncedSync implements debouncing logic for a session
   881→// Always skips HEAD check since we know content just changed in autosave mode
   882→func (syncMgr *SyncManager) debouncedSync(sessionID, mdPath, mdContent string, rawData []byte, agentName string) {
   883→	// Get or create debounce state for this session
   884→	stateInterface, _ := syncMgr.debounceSessions.LoadOrStore(sessionID, &sessionDebounceState{})
   885→	state := stateInterface.(*sessionDebounceState)
   886→
   887→	state.mu.Lock()
   888→	defer state.mu.Unlock()
   889→
   890→	now := time.Now()
   891→	timeSinceLastSync := now.Sub(state.lastSyncTime)
   892→
   893→	// Check if we can sync immediately (no recent sync or first sync)
   894→	if state.lastSyncTime.IsZero() || timeSinceLastSync >= syncMgr.debounceInterval {
   895→		// Sync immediately
   896→		state.lastSyncTime = now
   897→		state.pending = nil
   898→
   899→		// Cancel any pending timer
   900→		if state.timer != nil {
   901→			state.timer.Stop()
   902→			state.timer = nil
   903→		}
   904→
   905→		// Start sync in goroutine (always skip HEAD check in autosave mode)
   906→		syncMgr.wg.Add(1)
   907→		atomic.AddInt32(&syncMgr.syncCount, 1)
   908→		go func() {
   909→			defer func() {
   910→				syncMgr.wg.Done()
   911→				atomic.AddInt32(&syncMgr.syncCount, -1)
   912→			}()
   913→			syncMgr.performSync(sessionID, mdPath, mdContent, rawData, agentName, true)
   914→		}()
   915→
   916→		// Log with timeSinceLastSync only if meaningful (not after cleanup)
   917→		if state.lastSyncTime.IsZero() {
   918→			slog.Debug("Cloud sync started immediately",
   919→				"sessionId", sessionID)
   920→		} else {
   921→			slog.Debug("Cloud sync started immediately",
   922→				"sessionId", sessionID,
   923→				"timeSinceLastSync", timeSinceLastSync)
   924→		}
   925→		return
   926→	}
   927→
   928→	// Within debounce window - queue or replace pending request
   929→	state.pending = &pendingSyncRequest{
   930→		sessionID: sessionID,
   931→		mdPath:    mdPath,
   932→		mdContent: mdContent,
   933→		rawData:   rawData,
   934→		agentName: agentName,
   935→	}
   936→
   937→	// Set timer if not already set
   938→	if state.timer == nil {
   939→		timeUntilSync := syncMgr.debounceInterval - timeSinceLastSync
   940→		state.timer = time.AfterFunc(timeUntilSync, func() {
   941→			syncMgr.flushPendingSync(sessionID)
   942→		})
   943→
   944→		slog.Debug("Cloud sync queued with new timer",
   945→			"sessionId", sessionID,
   946→			"timeUntilSync", timeUntilSync)
   947→	} else {
   948→		slog.Debug("Cloud sync queued (replaced pending)",
   949→			"sessionId", sessionID)
   950→	}
   951→}
   952→
   953→// flushPendingSync is called by timer to flush a queued sync
   954→func (syncMgr *SyncManager) flushPendingSync(sessionID string) {
   955→	stateInterface, ok := syncMgr.debounceSessions.Load(sessionID)
   956→	if !ok {
   957→		return
   958→	}
   959→	state := stateInterface.(*sessionDebounceState)
   960→
   961→	state.mu.Lock()
   962→	defer state.mu.Unlock()
   963→
   964→	// Sync the pending request (timer is only set when pending is non-nil)
   965→	req := state.pending
   966→	state.pending = nil
   967→	state.timer = nil
   968→
   969→	// Guard against nil pending request (defensive programming)
   970→	if req == nil {
   971→		slog.Warn("Timer fired but no pending request found",
   972→			"sessionId", sessionID)
   973→		syncMgr.debounceSessions.Delete(sessionID)
   974→		return
   975→	}
   976→
   977→	// Clean up the session state from the map since we're done with this debounce cycle
   978→	// If another event comes later, LoadOrStore will create a fresh state
   979→	syncMgr.debounceSessions.Delete(sessionID)
   980→
   981→	// Start sync in goroutine (always skip HEAD check for debounced syncs)
   982→	syncMgr.wg.Add(1)
   983→	atomic.AddInt32(&syncMgr.syncCount, 1)
   984→	go func() {
   985→		defer func() {
   986→			syncMgr.wg.Done()
   987→			atomic.AddInt32(&syncMgr.syncCount, -1)
   988→		}()
   989→		syncMgr.performSync(req.sessionID, req.mdPath, req.mdContent, req.rawData, req.agentName, true)
   990→	}()
   991→
   992→	slog.Debug("Flushed pending sync after debounce, cleaned up session state",
   993→		"sessionId", sessionID)
   994→}
   995→
   996→// flushAllPending flushes all pending debounced syncs (called on shutdown)
   997→func (syncMgr *SyncManager) flushAllPending() {
   998→	slog.Debug("Flushing all pending debounced syncs")
   999→
  1000→	syncMgr.debounceSessions.Range(func(key, value interface{}) bool {
  1001→		sessionID := key.(string)
  1002→		state := value.(*sessionDebounceState)
  1003→
  1004→		state.mu.Lock()
  1005→		defer state.mu.Unlock()
  1006→
  1007→		// Cancel timer
  1008→		if state.timer != nil {
  1009→			state.timer.Stop()
  1010→			state.timer = nil
  1011→		}
  1012→
  1013→		// If there's a pending request, sync it immediately
  1014→		if state.pending != nil {
  1015→			req := state.pending
  1016→			state.pending = nil
  1017→
  1018→			// Start sync in goroutine (always skip HEAD check for debounced syncs)
  1019→			syncMgr.wg.Add(1)
  1020→			atomic.AddInt32(&syncMgr.syncCount, 1)
  1021→			go func() {
  1022→				defer func() {
  1023→					syncMgr.wg.Done()
  1024→					atomic.AddInt32(&syncMgr.syncCount, -1)
  1025→				}()
  1026→				syncMgr.performSync(req.sessionID, req.mdPath, req.mdContent, req.rawData, req.agentName, true)
  1027→			}()
  1028→
  1029→			slog.Info("Flushing pending sync on shutdown",
  1030→				"sessionId", sessionID)
  1031→		}
  1032→
  1033→		return true
  1034→	})
  1035→}
  1036→
  1037→// SyncSessionToCloud asynchronously syncs a session to the cloud
  1038→// When isAutosaveMode is true (run command), syncs are debounced and skip HEAD checks for efficiency
  1039→// When isAutosaveMode is false (manual sync), syncs are immediate with HEAD checks
  1040→func SyncSessionToCloud(sessionID string, mdPath string, mdContent string, rawData []byte, agentName string, isAutosaveMode bool) {
  1041→	syncManagerMutex.RLock()
  1042→	syncMgr := globalSyncManager
  1043→	syncManagerMutex.RUnlock()
  1044→
  1045→	if syncMgr == nil || !syncMgr.enabled {
  1046→		if syncMgr == nil {
  1047→			slog.Warn("Cloud sync not initialized, skipping sync", "sessionId", sessionID)
  1048→		} else {
  1049→			slog.Debug("Cloud sync disabled by flag, skipping sync", "sessionId", sessionID)
  1050→		}
  1051→		return
  1052→	}
  1053→
  1054→	// Check authentication
  1055→	if !IsAuthenticated() {
  1056→		slog.Warn("Cloud sync skipped: user not authenticated", "sessionId", sessionID)
  1057→		return
  1058→	}
  1059→
  1060→	// Route to debounced or immediate sync based on mode
  1061→	if isAutosaveMode {
  1062→		// Autosave mode: debounce syncs and skip HEAD checks
  1063→		syncMgr.debouncedSync(sessionID, mdPath, mdContent, rawData, agentName)
  1064→	} else {
  1065→		// Manual sync mode: immediate sync with HEAD check
  1066→		syncMgr.wg.Add(1)
  1067→		atomic.AddInt32(&syncMgr.syncCount, 1)
  1068→		go func() {
  1069→			defer func() {
  1070→				syncMgr.wg.Done()
  1071→				atomic.AddInt32(&syncMgr.syncCount, -1)
  1072→			}()
  1073→			syncMgr.performSync(sessionID, mdPath, mdContent, rawData, agentName, false)
  1074→		}()
  1075→	}
  1076→}
  1077→
  1078→// getStats loads atomic counters and returns a CloudSyncStats with calculated timeout count.
  1079→// This consolidates the pattern of loading counters + calculating timeouts used in multiple shutdown paths.
  1080→func (sm *SyncManager) getStats() *CloudSyncStats {
  1081→	stats := &CloudSyncStats{
  1082→		SessionsAttempted: atomic.LoadInt32(&sm.stats.SessionsAttempted),
  1083→		SessionsSkipped:   atomic.LoadInt32(&sm.stats.SessionsSkipped),
  1084→		SessionsUpdated:   atomic.LoadInt32(&sm.stats.SessionsUpdated),
  1085→		SessionsCreated:   atomic.LoadInt32(&sm.stats.SessionsCreated),
  1086→		SessionsErrored:   atomic.LoadInt32(&sm.stats.SessionsErrored),
  1087→	}
  1088→	stats.calculateTimedOut()
  1089→	return stats
  1090→}
  1091→
  1092→// Shutdown waits for all cloud sync operations to complete with a timeout and returns statistics
  1093→func Shutdown(timeout time.Duration) *CloudSyncStats {
  1094→	syncManagerMutex.RLock()
  1095→	sm := globalSyncManager
  1096→	syncManagerMutex.RUnlock()
  1097→
  1098→	if sm == nil || !sm.enabled {
  1099→		return nil
  1100→	}
  1101→
  1102→	// Flush all pending debounced syncs before waiting
  1103→	sm.flushAllPending()
  1104→
  1105→	// Create a channel to signal when all syncs are done
  1106→	done := make(chan struct{})
  1107→	go func() {
  1108→		sm.wg.Wait()
  1109→		close(done)
  1110→	}()
  1111→
  1112→	// Give goroutines a moment to start and check if we have any work
  1113→	time.Sleep(100 * time.Millisecond)
  1114→
  1115→	// Check if all work is already done (e.g., all sessions were skipped)
  1116→	select {
  1117→	case <-done:
  1118→		// All work completed very quickly (likely all skipped)
  1119→		stats := sm.getStats()
  1120→		// If we have stats, return them
  1121→		if stats.SessionsAttempted > 0 {
  1122→			return stats
  1123→		}
  1124→		// No work was done at all
  1125→		return nil
  1126→	default:
  1127→		// Work is still in progress, continue to show countdown
  1128→	}
  1129→
  1130→	// Create a ticker for countdown updates
  1131→	ticker := time.NewTicker(1 * time.Second)
  1132→	defer ticker.Stop()
  1133→
  1134→	// Track elapsed time
  1135→	start := time.Now()
  1136→
  1137→	// Show initial countdown immediately
  1138→	if !sm.silent {
  1139→		fmt.Printf("Waiting for cloud sync to complete... %ds until timeout", int(timeout.Seconds()))
  1140→	}
  1141→
  1142→	for {
  1143→		select {
  1144→		case <-done:
  1145→			// Ensure countdown is visible for at least 1 second
  1146→			elapsed := time.Since(start)
  1147→			if elapsed < 1*time.Second {
  1148→				time.Sleep(1*time.Second - elapsed)
  1149→			}
  1150→
  1151→			// All syncs completed - clear the progress line
  1152→			if !sm.silent {
  1153→				// Clear the line by printing spaces over it, then return to beginning
  1154→				fmt.Printf("\r%60s\r", "")
  1155→			}
  1156→
  1157→			// Return stats
  1158→			return sm.getStats()
  1159→		case <-ticker.C:
  1160→			// Update countdown
  1161→			elapsed := time.Since(start)
  1162→			remaining := timeout - elapsed
  1163→			if remaining <= 0 {
  1164→				// Timeout reached - clear the progress line
  1165→				if !sm.silent {
  1166→					fmt.Printf("\r%60s\r", "")
  1167→					fmt.Printf("Cloud sync timeout. Some syncs may not have completed.\n")
  1168→				}
  1169→				slog.Warn("Cloud sync shutdown timeout", "timeout", timeout)
  1170→
  1171→				// Return partial stats
  1172→				return sm.getStats()
  1173→			}
  1174→			// Show countdown
  1175→			if !sm.silent {
  1176→				fmt.Printf("\rWaiting for cloud sync to complete... %ds until timeout", int(remaining.Seconds()))
  1177→			}
  1178→		}
  1179→	}
  1180→}
  1181→
  1182→// readProjectConfig reads the .specstory/.project.json file
  1183→func readProjectConfig() (*ProjectData, error) {
  1184→	var projectData ProjectData
  1185→	var err error
  1186→
  1187→	// Read the project config file
  1188→	configPath := filepath.Join(utils.SPECSTORY_DIR, utils.PROJECT_JSON_FILE)
  1189→	data, err := os.ReadFile(configPath)
  1190→	if err != nil {
  1191→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to read project config: %w", err)
  1192→	}
  1193→
  1194→	// Parse JSON
  1195→	err = json.Unmarshal(data, &projectData)
  1196→	if err != nil {
  1197→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to parse project config: %w", err)
  1198→	}
  1199→
  1200→	return &projectData, nil
  1201→}

    24→// GenerateAgentSession creates a SessionData from a GeminiSession
    25→// The session should already be parsed and normalized (messages sorted, warmup trimmed)
    26→func GenerateAgentSession(session *GeminiSession, workspaceRoot string) (*SessionData, error) {
    27→	slog.Info("GenerateAgentSession: Starting", "sessionID", session.ID, "messageCount", len(session.Messages))
    28→
    29→	if len(session.Messages) == 0 {
    30→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("session has no messages")
    31→	}
    32→
    33→	// Use session's start time as createdAt
    34→	createdAt := session.StartTime
    35→	if createdAt == "" {
    36→		createdAt = session.LastUpdated
    37→	}
    38→
    39→	// Build exchanges from messages
    40→	exchanges, err := buildExchangesFromMessages(session.Messages, workspaceRoot)
    41→	if err != nil {
    42→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to build exchanges: %w", err)
    43→	}
    44→
    45→	// Assign exchangeId to each exchange (format: sessionId:index)
    46→	for i := range exchanges {
    47→		exchanges[i].ExchangeID = fmt.Sprintf("%s:%d", session.ID, i)
    48→	}
    49→
    50→	slog.Info("GenerateAgentSession: Built exchanges", "count", len(exchanges))
    51→
    52→	// Populate FormattedMarkdown for all tools
    53→	for i := range exchanges {
    54→		for j := range exchanges[i].Messages {
    55→			msg := &exchanges[i].Messages[j]
    56→			if msg.Tool != nil {
    57→				formattedMd := formatToolAsMarkdown(msg.Tool)
    58→				msg.Tool.FormattedMarkdown = &formattedMd
    59→			}
    60→		}
    61→	}
    62→
    63→	sessionData := &SessionData{
    64→		SchemaVersion: "1.0",
    65→		Provider: ProviderInfo{
    66→			ID:      "gemini-cli",
    67→			Name:    "Gemini CLI",
    68→			Version: "unknown",
    69→		},
    70→		SessionID:     session.ID,
    71→		CreatedAt:     createdAt,
    72→		WorkspaceRoot: workspaceRoot,
    73→		Exchanges:     exchanges,
    74→	}
    75→
    76→	return sessionData, nil
    77→}
    78→
    79→// buildExchangesFromMessages groups GeminiMessages into exchanges
    80→// An exchange starts with a user message and includes all subsequent agent messages until the next user message
    81→func buildExchangesFromMessages(messages []GeminiMessage, workspaceRoot string) ([]Exchange, error) {
    82→	var exchanges []Exchange
    83→	var currentExchange *Exchange
    84→
    85→	for _, msg := range messages {
    86→		switch msg.Type {
    87→		case "user":
    88→			// Start a new exchange
    89→			if currentExchange != nil && len(currentExchange.Messages) > 0 {
    90→				exchanges = append(exchanges, *currentExchange)
    91→			}
    92→
    93→			currentExchange = &Exchange{
    94→				StartTime: msg.Timestamp,
    95→				Messages:  []Message{},
    96→			}
    97→
    98→			// Build user message and handle referenced files extraction
    99→			userMsg, refMsgs := buildUserMessageWithReferences(msg)
   100→			currentExchange.Messages = append(currentExchange.Messages, userMsg)
   101→
   102→			// Add synthetic agent messages for referenced files
   103→			currentExchange.Messages = append(currentExchange.Messages, refMsgs...)
   104→
   105→		case "gemini":
   106→			// Agent message - add to current exchange
   107→			if currentExchange == nil {
   108→				// Create exchange if we don't have one (shouldn't happen normally)
   109→				currentExchange = &Exchange{
   110→					StartTime: msg.Timestamp,
   111→					Messages:  []Message{},
   112→				}
   113→			}
   114→
   115→			// Add messages for agent response (may produce multiple messages for tool calls)
   116→			agentMsgs := buildAgentMessages(msg, workspaceRoot)
   117→			currentExchange.Messages = append(currentExchange.Messages, agentMsgs...)
   118→			currentExchange.EndTime = msg.Timestamp
   119→		}
   120→	}
   121→
   122→	// Add the last exchange if exists
   123→	if currentExchange != nil && len(currentExchange.Messages) > 0 {
   124→		exchanges = append(exchanges, *currentExchange)
   125→	}
   126→
   127→	return exchanges, nil
   128→}
   129→
   130→// buildUserMessageWithReferences creates a user Message and extracts referenced file content
   131→// Returns the user message and any synthetic agent messages for referenced files
   132→func buildUserMessageWithReferences(msg GeminiMessage) (Message, []Message) {
   133→	content := strings.TrimSpace(msgContent(msg))
   134→
   135→	// Extract referenced file sections from user message
   136→	mainContent, refSections := extractReferencedSections(content)
   137→
   138→	var contentParts []ContentPart
   139→	if mainContent != "" {
   140→		contentParts = append(contentParts, ContentPart{
   141→			Type: "text",
   142→			Text: mainContent,
   143→		})
   144→	}
   145→
   146→	userMsg := Message{
   147→		ID:        msg.ID,
   148→		Timestamp: msg.Timestamp,
   149→		Role:      "user",
   150→		Content:   contentParts,
   151→	}
   152→
   153→	// Build synthetic agent messages for each referenced file section
   154→	var refMsgs []Message
   155→	for _, refContent := range refSections {
   156→		refMsg := buildReferencedFilesMessage(refContent, msg.Timestamp)
   157→		refMsgs = append(refMsgs, refMsg)
   158→	}
   159→
   160→	return userMsg, refMsgs
   161→}
   162→
   163→// buildReferencedFilesMessage creates a synthetic agent message for referenced file content
   164→func buildReferencedFilesMessage(content string, timestamp string) Message {
   165→	// Format the content with HTML escaping
   166→	formattedContent := formatReferencedFilesMarkdown(content)
   167→
   168→	tool := &ToolInfo{
   169→		Name:  "referenced_files",
   170→		Type:  "read",
   171→		UseID: fmt.Sprintf("ref_%s", timestamp),
   172→		Input: map[string]interface{}{
   173→			"content": content,
   174→		},
   175→		FormattedMarkdown: &formattedContent,
   176→	}
   177→
   178→	return Message{
   179→		Timestamp: timestamp,
   180→		Role:      "agent",
   181→		Tool:      tool,
   182→	}
   183→}
   184→
   185→// formatReferencedFilesMarkdown formats referenced file content for markdown display
   186→func formatReferencedFilesMarkdown(content string) string {
   187→	if content == "" {
   188→		return ""
   189→	}
   190→	return fmt.Sprintf("\n\n<pre><code>%s</code></pre>\n", html.EscapeString(content))
   191→}
   192→
   193→// buildAgentMessages creates Messages from a Gemini agent message
   194→// Returns multiple messages: one for thoughts, one per tool call, and one for text content
   195→func buildAgentMessages(msg GeminiMessage, workspaceRoot string) []Message {
   196→	var messages []Message
   197→
   198→	// Add thinking content from Thoughts
   199→	if len(msg.Thoughts) > 0 {
   200→		thinkingMsg := buildThinkingMessage(msg)
   201→		messages = append(messages, thinkingMsg)
   202→	}
   203→
   204→	// Add a message for each tool call
   205→	for _, toolCall := range msg.ToolCalls {
   206→		toolMsg := buildToolMessage(msg, toolCall, workspaceRoot)
   207→		messages = append(messages, toolMsg)
   208→	}
   209→
   210→	// Add text content message if present
   211→	textContent := strings.TrimSpace(msgContent(msg))
   212→	if textContent != "" {
   213→		textMsg := Message{
   214→			ID:        msg.ID,
   215→			Timestamp: msg.Timestamp,
   216→			Role:      "agent",
   217→			Model:     msg.Model,
   218→			Content: []ContentPart{
   219→				{Type: "text", Text: textContent},
   220→			},
   221→		}
   222→		messages = append(messages, textMsg)
   223→	}
   224→
   225→	return messages
   226→}
   227→
   228→// buildThinkingMessage creates a Message containing thinking content from GeminiThoughts
   229→func buildThinkingMessage(msg GeminiMessage) Message {
   230→	var thinkingText strings.Builder
   231→	for _, thought := range msg.Thoughts {
   232→		subject := strings.TrimSpace(thought.Subject)
   233→		if subject == "" {
   234→			subject = "Thought"
   235→		}
   236→		description := strings.TrimSpace(thought.Description)
   237→		if description == "" {
   238→			description = "(no description)"
   239→		}
   240→		fmt.Fprintf(&thinkingText, "- **%s** — %s\n", subject, description)
   241→	}
   242→
   243→	// Trim trailing newline to avoid double-newline when markdown adds closing tag
   244→	text := strings.TrimSuffix(thinkingText.String(), "\n")
   245→
   246→	return Message{
   247→		ID:        msg.ID,
   248→		Timestamp: msg.Timestamp,
   249→		Role:      "agent",
   250→		Model:     msg.Model,
   251→		Content: []ContentPart{
   252→			{Type: "thinking", Text: text},
   253→		},
   254→	}
   255→}
   256→
   257→// buildToolMessage creates a Message for a single tool call
   258→func buildToolMessage(msg GeminiMessage, toolCall GeminiToolCall, workspaceRoot string) Message {
   259→	// Convert GeminiToolCall to ToolInfo
   260→	toolInfo := convertToolCall(toolCall)
   261→
   262→	// Extract path hints from tool input
   263→	pathHints := extractPathHintsFromTool(toolCall, workspaceRoot)
   264→
   265→	return Message{
   266→		ID:        toolCall.ID,
   267→		Timestamp: toolCall.Timestamp,
   268→		Role:      "agent",
   269→		Model:     msg.Model,
   270→		Tool:      toolInfo,
   271→		PathHints: pathHints,
   272→	}
   273→}
   274→
   275→// convertToolCall converts a GeminiToolCall to a ToolInfo
   276→func convertToolCall(toolCall GeminiToolCall) *ToolInfo {
   277→	// Build output from tool results
   278→	var output map[string]interface{}
   279→	if len(toolCall.Result) > 0 && toolCall.Result[0].FunctionResponse != nil {
   280→		output = toolCall.Result[0].FunctionResponse.Response
   281→	}
   282→
   283→	// If no structured output, try ResultDisplay
   284→	if output == nil {
   285→		if display := resultDisplayString(toolCall.ResultDisplay); display != "" {
   286→			output = map[string]interface{}{
   287→				"output": display,
   288→			}
   289→		}
   290→	}
   291→
   292→	return &ToolInfo{
   293→		Name:   toolCall.Name,
   294→		Type:   classifyGeminiToolType(toolCall.Name),
   295→		UseID:  toolCall.ID,
   296→		Input:  toolCall.Args,
   297→		Output: output,
   298→	}
   299→}
   300→
   301→// classifyGeminiToolType maps Gemini tool names to standard tool types
   302→// Valid types: write, read, search, shell, task, generic, unknown
   303→func classifyGeminiToolType(toolName string) string {
   304→	switch toolName {
   305→	case "read_file", "read_many_files", "web_fetch":
   306→		return "read"
   307→	case "write_file", "replace", "smart_edit":
   308→		return "write"
   309→	case "run_shell_command", "list_directory":
   310→		return "shell"
   311→	case "search_file_content", "google_web_search", "glob":
   312→		return "search"
   313→	case "write_todos":
   314→		return "task"
   315→	case "save_memory", "codebase_investigator", "delegate_to_agent":
   316→		return "generic"
   317→	default:
   318→		return "unknown"
   319→	}
   320→}
   321→
   322→// extractPathHintsFromTool extracts file paths from Gemini tool call args
   323→func extractPathHintsFromTool(toolCall GeminiToolCall, workspaceRoot string) []string {
   324→	var paths []string
   325→
   326→	// Common path field names for Gemini tools
   327→	pathFields := []string{"file_path", "dir_path", "path"}
   328→
   329→	for _, field := range pathFields {
   330→		if value := inputAsString(toolCall.Args, field); value != "" {
   331→			normalizedPath := spi.NormalizePath(value, workspaceRoot)
   332→			if !slices.Contains(paths, normalizedPath) {
   333→				paths = append(paths, normalizedPath)
   334→			}
   335→		}
   336→	}
   337→
   338→	// Extract paths from shell commands (redirect targets, file-creating commands)
   339→	if command := inputAsString(toolCall.Args, "command"); command != "" {
   340→		cwd := inputAsString(toolCall.Args, "dir_path")
   341→		if cwd == "" {
   342→			cwd = workspaceRoot
   343→		}
   344→		shellPaths := spi.ExtractShellPathHints(command, cwd, workspaceRoot)
   345→		for _, sp := range shellPaths {
   346→			if !slices.Contains(paths, sp) {
   347→				paths = append(paths, sp)
   348→			}
   349→		}
   350→	}
   351→
   352→	return paths
   353→}
   354→
   355→// extractReferencedSections extracts "--- Content from referenced files ---" sections from a message
   356→// Returns the main content (without markers) and the extracted reference sections
   357→func extractReferencedSections(message string) (string, []string) {
   358→	const marker = "--- Content from referenced files ---"
   359→	var sections []string
   360→	remaining := message
   361→
   362→	for {
   363→		idx := strings.Index(remaining, marker)
   364→		if idx == -1 {
   365→			break
   366→		}
   367→		before := strings.TrimRight(remaining[:idx], "\n ")
   368→		after := remaining[idx+len(marker):]
   369→
   370→		next := strings.Index(after, marker)
   371→		var block string
   372→		if next == -1 {
   373→			block = strings.TrimSpace(after)
   374→			remaining = before
   375→		} else {
   376→			block = strings.TrimSpace(after[:next])
   377→			remaining = strings.TrimSpace(before + after[next:])
   378→		}
   379→
   380→		if block != "" {
   381→			sections = append(sections, block)
   382→		}
   383→	}
   384→
   385→	return strings.TrimSpace(remaining), sections
   386→}
   387→
```

    33→// Properties is a type alias for event properties to avoid exposing PostHog types

    36→// TrackEvent sends a generic event to PostHog
    37→func TrackEvent(eventName string, properties Properties) {

    40→	}
    41→
    42→	distinctID := GetDistinctID()
    43→

    52→	phProperties["$device_id"] = distinctID
    53→
    54→	// Capture current working directory as project_path

    57→	}

    59→	// Agent provider information

    61→
    62→	// Editor information
    63→	phProperties["editor_name"] = "SpecStory CLI"
    64→	phProperties["editor_type"] = "CLI"
    65→	phProperties["extension_version"] = GetCLIVersion()
    66→
    67→	// OS information (gathered once during initialization)
    68→	osArch, osName, osPlatform, osVersion := GetOSInfo()
    69→	phProperties["os_arch"] = osArch
    70→	phProperties["os_name"] = osName
    71→	phProperties["os_platform"] = osPlatform
    72→	phProperties["os_version"] = osVersion
    73→
    74→	err := client.Enqueue(posthog.Capture{
    75→		DistinctId:       distinctID,
    76→		Event:            eventName,
    77→		Properties:       phProperties,
    78→		SendFeatureFlags: posthog.SendFeatureFlags(false),
    79→	})
    80→
    81→	if err != nil {
    82→		// Silently fail - analytics should not break the app
    83→		return
    84→	}
    85→}

// AgentChatSession represents a chat session from an AI coding agent
type AgentChatSession struct {

	CreatedAt   string              // Stable ISO 8601 timestamp when session was created
	Slug        string              // Stable human-readable but file name safe slug, often derived from first user message
	SessionData *schema.SessionData // Structured session data in unified format

## Code to Count User Messages

To count user prompts from session data, you would implement something like this pattern (based on the existing schema):

```go
// Iterate through exchanges and messages
for _, exchange := range sessionData.Exchanges {
	for _, msg := range exchange.Messages {
		if msg.Role == schema.RoleUser {  // Check against the "user" constant
			userCount++
		}
	}

## PR #170: Watch Session Updates — Code Review

### Line-by-Line Review

```
main.go
```

**Line 510 (modified):** Changed `err :=` to `_, err :=` to discard new return value from `processSingleSession`
- (1) ✅ Correct — the `run` command doesn't need the markdown size, ignoring it is appropriate

---

**Lines 691-700 (new):** Pre-check whether markdown file exists before calling `processSingleSession`

```go
timestamp, _ := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, session.CreatedAt)
timestampStr := formatFilenameTimestamp(timestamp, useUTC)
filename := timestampStr
if session.Slug != "" {
    filename = fmt.Sprintf("%s-%s", timestampStr, session.Slug)
}
fileFullPath := filepath.Join(config.GetHistoryDir(), filename+".md")
_, fileExistsErr := os.Stat(fileFullPath)
fileExists := fileExistsErr == nil
```

- (2) ⚠️ **DRY violation — this is the third copy of the filename construction logic.** The exact same timestamp-parse → `formatFilenameTimestamp` → slug-append → `filepath.Join` pattern already exists in `processSingleSession()` (lines 1141-1149) and `syncProvider()` (lines 1365-1373). This should be extracted into a shared helper.
- (3) ⚠️ **Race condition / inaccuracy risk.** The file existence check happens *before* `processSingleSession` runs. But `processSingleSession` is the function that actually writes the file. If the file didn't exist, but another concurrent goroutine (from a different provider) writes it between the `os.Stat` check and `processSingleSession`, the "created"/"updated" label would be wrong. Admittedly this is unlikely for a single session, but the pre-check is also just unnecessary — `processSingleSession` already determines `fileExists` internally (line 1158-1160) and could return it.
- (4) ⚠️ The `_` on `time.Parse` silently discards parse errors. This is consistent with existing code, but worth noting — a malformed `CreatedAt` would produce a zero-time filename here that differs from what `processSingleSession` produces (they'd match because both discard the error, but it's fragile).

---

**Line 705 (modified):** Captures `markdownSize` return from `processSingleSession`
- (5) ✅ Good — this is the primary data needed for the output line

---

**Lines 715-716 (new):** Added `return` after error logging in the watch callback

```go
    return
}
```

- (6) ✅ **Good bug fix / behavioral improvement.** Previously, after an error in `processSingleSession`, execution would fall through to `processProvenanceEvents`. Now it correctly returns early. This is a genuine improvement beyond the feature scope.

---

**Lines 719-724 (new):** Determine created vs updated action string

```go
if !silent {
    action := "updated"
    if !fileExists {
        action = "created"
    }
```

- (7) ✅ Variable name `action` is clear and appropriate
- (8) ✅ Defaulting to "updated" and overriding to "created" is readable

---

**Lines 726-733 (new):** Get start and end timestamps

```go
startTime := session.CreatedAt
endTime := ""
if session.SessionData != nil && session.SessionData.UpdatedAt != "" {
    endTime = session.SessionData.UpdatedAt
} else {
    endTime = startTime
}
```

- (9) ✅ Good nil check on `SessionData`
- (10) ⚠️ Minor: The `endTime := ""` initialization is immediately overwritten in both branches of the if/else, so it's dead code. Could simplify to just the if/else without the initial assignment, or use a single expression.

---

**Lines 735-743 (new):** Reverse-lookup the provider ID from the registry

```go
providerID := ""
registry := factory.GetRegistry()
for _, id := range registry.ListIDs() {
    if prov, _ := registry.Get(id); prov == p {
        providerID = id
        break
    }
}
```

- (11) ⚠️ **The provider ID is already known in the enclosing scope.** The `for _, id := range providerIDs` loop at line 678 iterates over `providerIDs`, and `provider := providers[id]` at line 679 maps ID to provider. The goroutine closure captures `p spi.Provider` but the `id` is right there. This entire registry reverse-lookup is unnecessary. The goroutine signature could be changed to `go func(p spi.Provider, providerID string)` and passed the `id` directly, or it could just be captured from the loop variable.
- (12) ⚠️ Pointer equality comparison (`prov == p`) for matching providers is brittle. It works because the registry returns singleton instances, but this is an implicit assumption.

---

**Lines 745-755 (new):** Count user prompts by iterating all exchanges and messages

```go
userPrompts := 0
if session.SessionData != nil {
    for _, exchange := range session.SessionData.Exchanges {
        for _, msg := range exchange.Messages {
            if msg.Role == "user" {
                userPrompts++
            }
        }
    }
}
```

- (13) ⚠️ **Should use the `schema.RoleUser` constant** instead of the string literal `"user"`. The codebase defines `RoleUser = "user"` in `pkg/spi/schema/types.go` and uses it throughout the schema package. The existing `pkg/markdown/markdown.go:142` also uses the string literal, so this is a pre-existing inconsistency, but new code should use the constant.
- (14) ✅ Good nil guard on `SessionData`
- (15) ❓ For large sessions with many exchanges, this nested loop runs on every callback invocation (which can fire frequently during an active session). The cost is likely negligible, but worth noting.

---

**Lines 757-766 (new):** Format and print the output line

```go
fmt.Printf("(%s) Session %s (%s): start time %s, end time %s, provider %s, markdown size %d, total user prompts %d\n",
    time.Now().Format(time.RFC3339),
    action,
    session.SessionID,
    startTime,
    endTime,
    providerID,
    markdownSize,
    userPrompts)
```

- (16) ✅ Output format matches the PR description exactly
- (17) ⚠️ **Uses `fmt.Printf` to stdout.** This is intentional (consumed by the extension), but in a concurrent goroutine (one per provider), multiple providers could interleave their output. Since `fmt.Printf` is not atomic for long strings, this could produce garbled output with concurrent providers. A mutex or buffered write would be safer, though in practice there's likely only one provider producing output at a time.
- (18) ❓ **No analytics tracking** for this new watch session event. Other parts of the codebase track session creation/update events (e.g., `EventAutosaveNew`, `EventAutosaveSuccess`). Should this new watch output be tracked as a distinct event, or is the existing autosave tracking in `processSingleSession` sufficient?
- (19) ⚠️ Missing comment explaining *why* this output exists (i.e., "consumed by the VS Code extension to update in-memory statistics"). The PR description explains this, but the code doesn't.

---

**Lines 1051 (modified):** Changed `err :=` to `_, err :=` in sync command

```go
if _, err := processSingleSession(session, sessionProvider, config, true, false, debugRaw, useUTC); err != nil {
```

- (20) ✅ Correct — sync command doesn't need markdown size

---

**Lines 1126-1127 (modified):** Updated function signature and doc comment

```go
// Returns the size of the markdown content in bytes
func processSingleSession(...) (int, error) {
```

- (21) ✅ Good — doc comment updated to describe the new return value
- (22) ✅ Return type `int` is appropriate for `len(string)` which returns byte count

---

**Line 1135 (modified):** `return 0, fmt.Errorf(...)` — error return updated
- (23) ✅ Correct zero-value for size on error

---

**Lines 1138-1139 (new):** Calculate markdown size

```go
// Calculate markdown size in bytes
markdownSize := len(markdownContent)
```

- (24) ⚠️ The comment "Calculate markdown size in bytes" is a "what" comment, not a "why" comment. The code is self-evident. The comment should either be removed or explain *why* we need the size (e.g., "returned to caller for watch mode output").
- (25) ✅ Placement is correct — calculated right after generation, before any early returns

---

**Lines 1174, 1192 (modified):** Error returns updated to `return 0, fmt.Errorf(...)`
- (26) ✅ Mechanical changes, correct zero-values on error paths

---

**Line 1258 (modified):** Success return updated to `return markdownSize, nil`
- (27) ✅ Correct — returns the calculated size on success

---

### Top Improvements

**1. Eliminate the duplicated filename construction (observation 2).** The timestamp-parse → format → slug-append → join pattern now appears in three places. Extract a helper like `buildSessionFilePath(session *spi.AgentChatSession, historyDir string, useUTC bool) string`. This is the single biggest code quality issue.

**2. Pass the provider ID directly instead of the registry reverse-lookup (observation 11).** The `id` is already available in the enclosing `for` loop at line 678. Change the goroutine to `go func(p spi.Provider, providerID string)` and pass it. This removes ~8 lines of unnecessary code and eliminates the pointer equality concern (observation 12).

**3. Use `schema.RoleUser` constant instead of the `"user"` string literal (observation 13).** This is a small change but aligns with the project's own type definitions.

**4. Add a "why" comment explaining the stdout output purpose (observation 19).** Something like `// Structured output consumed by VS Code extension for live session statistics` would help future maintainers understand why this is `fmt.Printf` to stdout rather than `slog.Info`.

**5. Consider having `processSingleSession` return a result struct instead of growing positional returns.** Right now it returns `(int, error)`. If the watch command also needs `fileExists` (to determine created vs. updated), adding yet another return value would be messy. A small struct like `type ProcessResult struct { MarkdownSize int; FileCreated bool }` would keep the API clean and eliminate the duplicated file-existence pre-check (observation 3).

---

### Unit Test Assessment

No new test files or test functions were added in this PR.

**Should there be tests?**

- **Filename construction helper** — If the duplicated filename code is extracted into a helper (improvement #1), that helper deserves a unit test with table-driven cases covering: empty slug, non-empty slug, UTC vs local, and malformed timestamps. The `formatFilenameTimestamp` function already has tests, but the slug-append + path-join logic does not.

- **User prompt counting** — If the user prompt counting logic is extracted into a helper (reasonable since it's a distinct piece of logic), it would benefit from table-driven tests covering: nil SessionData, empty exchanges, exchanges with no user messages, exchanges with mixed roles, and multiple exchanges. This has genuine combinatorial complexity.

- **The `fmt.Printf` output line itself** — Testing the exact output format is probably too brittle and tautological. The format is simple string interpolation. Not worth testing.

- **The `processSingleSession` return value** — The existing tests (if any) for `processSingleSession` should be updated to capture and verify the new `int` return, but only if `processSingleSession` already has unit tests. Given it's a large integration-style function with file I/O and cloud sync calls, it may not be directly unit-testable, which is fine.

---

### 1. Watch Command Flags - Exact Cobra Definitions

**Location:** `/Users/sean/Source/SpecStory/getspecstory/specstory-cli/main.go`, lines 1782-1791

```go
watchCmd.Flags().BoolVar(&provenanceEnabled, "provenance", false, "enable AI provenance tracking (correlate file changes to agent activity)")
_ = watchCmd.Flags().MarkHidden("provenance") // Hidden flag
watchCmd.Flags().StringVar(&outputDir, "output-dir", "", "custom output directory for markdown and debug files (default: ./.specstory/history)")
watchCmd.Flags().BoolVar(&noCloudSync, "no-cloud-sync", false, "disable cloud sync functionality")
watchCmd.Flags().BoolVar(&onlyCloudSync, "only-cloud-sync", false, "skip local markdown file saves, only upload to cloud (requires authentication)")
watchCmd.Flags().StringVar(&cloudURL, "cloud-url", "", "override the default cloud API base URL")
_ = watchCmd.Flags().MarkHidden("cloud-url") // Hidden flag
watchCmd.Flags().Bool("debug-raw", false, "debug mode to output pretty-printed raw data files")
_ = watchCmd.Flags().MarkHidden("debug-raw") // Hidden flag
watchCmd.Flags().BoolP("local-time-zone", "", false, "use local timezone for file name and content timestamps (when not present: UTC)")
```

**Key boolean flags:**
- `--silent` is a global/persistent flag (line 1752)
- `--only-cloud-sync` (line 1786)
- `--no-cloud-sync` (line 1785)
- `--provenance` (line 1782, hidden)
- `--debug-raw` (line 1789, hidden)
- `--local-time-zone` (line 1791)

The `--silent` flag is defined globally at line 1752 and applies to all commands:
```go
rootCmd.PersistentFlags().BoolVar(&silent, "silent", false, "suppress all non-error output")
```

### 2. `--json` Flag Pattern in Codebase

**Location:** `/Users/sean/Source/SpecStory/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/cmd/list.go`

The `--json` pattern is currently **only used in the `list` command**, not in watch, run, or sync:

```go
// Line 31-32: Type definition
type listFlags struct {
	json bool
}

// Line 96: Flag registration
cmd.Flags().BoolVar(&flags.json, "json", false, "Output as JSON (default is human-readable table)")
```

**Usage pattern in `list.go`:**
```go
// Line 251-260: Conditional output based on flag
if flags.json {
    encoder := json.NewEncoder(os.Stdout)
    encoder.SetIndent("", "  ")
    if err := encoder.Encode(sessions); err != nil {
        return fmt.Errorf("failed to encode sessions as JSON: %w", err)
    }
} else {
    printSessionTable(sessions)
}
```

There is **no existing `--json` flag in the watch command**. This is a new pattern you'd be introducing.

### 3. Watch Session Callback Printf Output Block

**Location:** `/Users/sean/Source/SpecStory/getspecstory/specstory-cli/main.go`, lines 713-750

Here's the complete formatted output block:

```go
// Output formatted line to stdout for watch mode
if !silent {
    // Determine if this was an update or creation
    action := "updated"
    if !fileExists {
        action = "created"
    }

    // Get timestamps from session data
    startTime := session.CreatedAt
    endTime := startTime
    if session.SessionData != nil && session.SessionData.UpdatedAt != "" {
        endTime = session.SessionData.UpdatedAt
    }

    // Count user prompts/messages
    userPrompts := 0
    if session.SessionData != nil {
        for _, exchange := range session.SessionData.Exchanges {
            for _, msg := range exchange.Messages {
                if msg.Role == schema.RoleUser {
                    userPrompts++
                }
            }
        }
    }

    // Output the formatted line
    fmt.Printf("(%s) Session %s (%s): start time %s, end time %s, provider %s, markdown size %d, total user prompts %d\n",
        time.Now().Format(time.RFC3339),
        action,
        session.SessionID,
        startTime,
        endTime,
        providerID,
        markdownSize,
        userPrompts)
}
```

**The `if !silent` guard (line 714) controls all output.** When `--silent` is enabled, nothing is printed to stdout in watch mode.

When `onlyCloudSync` is true:

2. **Always triggers cloud sync (line 1238):**

### 5. `buildSessionFilePath` Helper Function

**Location:** `/Users/sean/Source/SpecStory/getspecstory/specstory-cli/main.go`, lines 1105-1116

```go
// buildSessionFilePath constructs the full markdown file path for a session.
// All callers need consistent filename generation from session metadata.
func buildSessionFilePath(session *spi.AgentChatSession, historyDir string, useUTC bool) string {
    timestamp, _ := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, session.CreatedAt)
    timestampStr := formatFilenameTimestamp(timestamp, useUTC)

    filename := timestampStr
    if session.Slug != "" {
        filename = fmt.Sprintf("%s-%s", timestampStr, session.Slug)
    }
    return filepath.Join(historyDir, filename+".md")
}
```

**What it returns:**
- Full absolute path to the markdown file: `{historyDir}/{timestamp}-{slug}.md` (if slug exists) or `{historyDir}/{timestamp}.md`
- Example: `/Users/sean/.specstory/history/2026-02-20_22-54-02Z-my-session.md`

**How it's called in watch:**
- Line 693: `fileFullPath := buildSessionFilePath(session, config.GetHistoryDir(), useUTC)`
- This path is used to check if the file already exists (lines 694-695)
- And passed to `processSingleSession` which uses it for file writing (line 1138)

**Filename format depends on `useUTC` flag:**
- Line 1094-1103 shows `formatFilenameTimestamp` logic:
  - UTC: `"2006-01-02_15-04-05Z"`
  - Local: `"2006-01-02_15-04-05-0700"`

    58→// startProvenanceEngine creates and returns a provenance engine if --provenance is enabled.
    59→// Returns nil engine and nil cleanup if provenance is not enabled.
    60→// The caller must invoke the returned cleanup function (typically via defer).
    61→func startProvenanceEngine() (*provenance.Engine, func(), error) {
    62→	if !provenanceEnabled {
    63→		return nil, func() {}, nil
    64→	}
    65→
    66→	engine, err := provenance.NewEngine()
    67→	if err != nil {
    68→		return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to start provenance engine: %w", err)
    69→	}
    70→
    71→	cleanup := func() {
    72→		if closeErr := engine.Close(); closeErr != nil {
    73→			slog.Error("Failed to close provenance engine", "error", closeErr)
    74→		}
    75→		slog.Info("Provenance engine stopped")
    76→	}
    77→
    78→	return engine, cleanup, nil
    79→}
    80→
    81→// startProvenanceFSWatcher creates a filesystem watcher that pushes FileEvents
    82→// to the provenance engine for correlation with agent activity. Returns a cleanup
    83→// function the caller must invoke (typically via defer). If the engine is nil
    84→// (provenance disabled), returns a no-op cleanup.
    85→func startProvenanceFSWatcher(ctx context.Context, engine *provenance.Engine, rootDir string) (func(), error) {
    86→	if engine == nil {
    87→		return func() {}, nil
    88→	}
    89→
    90→	watcher, err := provenance.NewFSWatcher(engine, rootDir)
    91→	if err != nil {
    92→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to start provenance FS watcher: %w", err)
    93→	}
    94→
    95→	watcher.Start(ctx)
    96→	return watcher.Stop, nil
    97→}

   131→	}
   132→	if debug && !console && !logFile {
   133→		return utils.ValidationError{Message: "--debug requires either --console or --log to be specified"}

   135→	if onlyCloudSync && noCloudSync {
   136→		return utils.ValidationError{Message: "cannot use --only-cloud-sync and --no-cloud-sync together. These flags are mutually exclusive"}

   139→		return utils.ValidationError{Message: "cannot use --print and --only-cloud-sync together. These flags are mutually exclusive"}
   140→	}
   141→	if printToStdout && console {
   142→		return utils.ValidationError{Message: "cannot use --print and --console together. Console debug output would interleave with markdown on stdout"}
   143→	}
   144→	return nil
   145→}
